primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, en comptes consolidés, des modèles de comptabilité de couverture IFRS potentiellement applicables à une exposition de change liée à des dividendes intragroupe / à la créance intragroupe correspondante reconnue

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge-accounting eligibility, hedged-item and hedging-model questions; therefore IFRS 9 governs and IAS 39 is discarded from the candidate set.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is about hedge-accounting eligibility and model identification for foreign currency risk in consolidation, which is directly addressed by IFRS 9 and not limited to the residual IAS 39 portfolio fair value hedge carve-out.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern eligible hedged items in consolidation, foreign currency intragroup exceptions, and the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain when foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are not fully eliminated in consolidation and how related exchange differences are presented and recycled.

This interpretation clarifies the hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge model, including the hedged risk, hedging instrument location, effectiveness reference currency, and recycling.

peripheral_authority.reason: These are disclosure requirements for hedge accounting and do not help identify the peer accounting approaches.

treatment_families.family: Named hedge accounting relationship types for foreign currency risk exposures in consolidation
Intragroup foreign currency exposures that may enter hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements
Net investment hedge as a distinct foreign currency hedge model

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment as a distinct named hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.
